executive_summary: 10th consecutive poll of PRED-105 silent-ignore. Two new milestones both predicted by prior-run handoff: (1) the manual-commit drought has crossed the 7-day threshold (now 7.16d); (2) today's 2026-04-24T07:12Z ECM Daily Review auto-regen (1fd2aef) produced output identical on every metric that matters (registry_ratio_pct=94.5, confirmed=69, prospective=10, refined=4) to yesterday's regen, making this the THIRD consecutive pipeline-canonical auto-regen day. Three auto-regens holding the line moves the observation from "algorithmic policy" (2 auto-regens, possibly coincidence) to "clearly the steady-state behaviour of build_daily_review.py". Correct response per prior-run handoff: minimal counter increment inside FAIL-009.algorithmic_dimension (2→3) plus a note about 7-day drought crossing; do NOT refile a full numerical refresh. No other escalation triggers fired: no goalpost-move, no WIN-070, no drought-BREAKING manual commit, no canary signal.
kernel_of_truth: Strongest charitable reading is unchanged from prior run: build_daily_review.py is an input-driven summariser, not an arbiter; if the author writes no explicit label to status_history.json for a closed window, the script mechanically produces "no news" rather than "window expired." That is a legitimate design choice one auto-regen at a time. Steve-preference-opposing-consideration: at 3 consecutive auto-regens, the "one tick of silence could be coincidence" defence is substantially weaker. The pipeline has deterministic logic that is now deterministically producing a steady-state that happens to exclude PRED-105 from the failure tally. The author could move PRED-105 to the existing refined bucket at any time, would require a single manual edit to status_history.json, and chooses not to for the 7th day running. Three auto-regens is not "build_daily_review.py is an input-driven summariser" — it is "build_daily_review.py is silent on window-expiry AND the author's workflow is silent on window-expiry AND the intersection is being preserved intentionally across 7+ days of other continuous automation activity." Counter-defence: 7 days over a weekend is inside ordinary personal-project dormancy. Against: the author has continued to commit through the pipeline (11 auto-commits in the poll window, 392 snapshots in status_history.json, latest 2026-04-24T15:39:43Z) so the pipeline is live and the author has repeatedly touched the system; silence is selective, not blanket.

counter_considerations: Per user preference — opposing considerations: (1) Pipeline-canonical saturation defence. The prior 2-auto-regen pipeline-canonical observation might itself be sufficient; extending to 3 auto-regens arguably adds little scientific content and is borderline churn. Against: the move from "possibly coincidence" to "clearly steady-state" is itself the qualitative upgrade the prior-run handoff anticipated. The counter mechanism exists precisely to express this upgrade cheaply. (2) 7-day threshold inflation defence. The 7-day drought is only 4-5 hours past the 6-day crossing that happened silently between polls. Treating 7-day-crossed as a distinct milestone may exaggerate the cadence of escalation. Against: the pre-locked rubric set specific day-thresholds and 7-day was explicitly named. Documenting the crossing is fidelity to the rubric, not inflation. (3) Dorman-author defence. 7.16 days is inside the range of vacation-plus-bridge for a solo-author hobby project; the author has shipped large batch-commits after comparable silences before (2026-04-17 = 3 manual commits in 25 min). Against: the predictions.html is byte-identical across 9 polls AND 11 auto-commits continue to ship. The author is engaging with automation but not with manual adjudication; that selective silence is the observation, not the total silence. (4) Newsworthy-threshold defence. 3rd consecutive pipeline-canonical auto-regen and 7-day drought crossing are still jargon for non-specialists. Newsworthy flag remains DEFERRED — the first manual commit that routes around PRED-105 (if it happens) would be the newsworthy moment. (5) Risk of fatigue from repeated PRED-105 churn in the pipeline. The decider has already closed ISS-1460 and ISS-1474 and still has ISS-1423 open for this pattern. Filing a third iteration within 72h risks looking like an analyst infinite-loop. Mitigation: combine counter increment + 7-day note into ONE issue-proposal, scoped tightly, offering both patches as a single atomic update that the decider can apply once and close.
gotchas: Use a combined issue-proposal with BOTH patches (counter increment AND 7-day-threshold note) — do NOT file two separate proposals. The decider is already fatigued on PRED-105 churn.
Do NOT reconcile FAIL-009 — reconciled_at 2026-04-20 stands. These are all additive/incremental updates, not reconciliation events.

Do NOT create a new ISS — ISS-1423 already captures silent-ignore + pipeline-canonical. The decider owns open-issues.json; we just supply the FAIL-009 patches via issue-proposal and let the decider slot them under the existing ISS or their chosen issue.

Do NOT conflate PRED-105 and PRED-072 patterns. PRED-072 TTB diurnal silent-ignore is now ~20d+ but has its own FAIL-010 tracking. The pipeline-canonical observation was detected specifically on PRED-105 in this poll; generalising across FAIL-010 requires independent confirmation.
The "7-day threshold CROSSED" wording is correct — not "broken", which would mean a manual commit occurred.
Do NOT repeat the structural pipeline-canonical prose inside the counter update. The existing field already explains the mechanism; only the count and the commit list should change.
